name: A Kid-Friendly Chef Salad
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound cubed cooked ham or chicken breast
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the toppings for this chef's salad. For hard-boiled eggs, place 2-4 eggs in a heavy saucepan and cover with cold water. Bring to a boil, then turn off the heat. Let sit covered for 15 minutes.
2. Drain hot water from eggs. Rinse eggs under running tap or in bowl of water. Slice into wedges.
3. For garlic croutons, cut 2 slices whole wheat bread into 16 squares each. Melt butter in microwave-safe bowl (40 seconds). Stir in garlic powder and toss with bread cubes. Arrange on baking sheet/toaster oven tray.
4. Broil for 1-2 minutes, checking frequently to prevent burning. Turn and brown other side. Drain on paper towel and cool.
5. For ranch-style dressing, whisk together sour cream, milk, vinegar, garlic powder, parsley flakes, dill, and a pinch of salt and pepper in small bowl. Chill at least 30 minutes while preparing remaining ingredients.
6. Prepare lettuce by cutting off end of romaine head and discarding. Break leaves off and rinse in cold water. Spin dry or pat with paper towels. Chop into bite-sized pieces and place in salad bowl.
7. Older kids can peel and grate carrot, wash, peel, and slice cucumber, and wash/cut pepper into strips or shapes. Wash tomatoes and remove stems.
8. Place ham or chicken, cheese, croutons, and hard-boiled eggs in bowls with dressing. Assemble salads on plates by hand. Serve with pitcher of dressing for dipping.
